content.caption: A scenic view of a beachside park with people sitting on benches and walking. There are palm trees and a white building in the background.
A social media post by Dhruv Batra announcing his departure from Meta after 8 years. He mentions leading FAIR's Embodied AI team and reflects on his work and future aspirations.
content.text:
12:27 1
Post
Dhruv Batra
@DhruvBatraDB
•
90
...
Update: After nearly 8 years, I have left Meta.
I was leading FAIR's Embodied Al team and I did
the best work of my life here.
My colleagues and I imagined a world where Al
agents can see, talk, and act. And we pulled that
future closer by building the required pieces -
visually-grounded chatbots for the digital world,
egocentric assistants for AR/SmartGlasses, and
robotic assistants for the physical world.
I continue to believe that the foundation of
intelligence is embodied sensorimotor control (not
ungrounded language). And I continue to be
excited about a future where every single human
interaction with the digital and physical world will
be mediated by a personal Al assistant. FAIR
Embodied Al is well-placed to deliver these
research breakthroughs.
So what's next for me?
Same convictions, new chapter, building
something new.
Post your reply
Q
☑
☑

events: Reflecting on work and future aspirations

semantic_knowledge: Dhruv Batra announced his departure from Meta after 8 years.
Dhruv Batra led FAIR's Embodied AI team at Meta.

content.caption: A screenshot of a social media post written in Chinese, discussing the importance of taste in scientific research and the challenges faced by researchers.
content.text:
11:04
Markov
100
Follow
☑
cnallenge,八刀石禾也都是以石兀八阿洋的反纸
meaningless也不必沮丧,毕竟留在教科书的solid
works本就不多,这部分经典也是依赖大量被淘汰的
paper做全面试错。
感觉学术研究和其他行业在个人发展这块也是有些共性
的,逻辑上都是先积极参与,involve in this game,做
些灌水和incremental works没关系,然后逐渐的能跟上
主流taste,最后幸运的话可以lead一个子方向(挖新
坑)
。
最后,真的感觉科研入门时候的taste建立太重要,这才
是最最依赖phd advisor的环节,知道什么是好工作,哪
怕现在做不出来,但总能不断接近。以及,千万别把学
术作为career的唯一途径,反正我现在心态就是学术训
练该做做该练练,能力达标机会合适就试试学术路线
(毕竟大多数读博的人应该都有这种动机才来),走不
了就拉倒,世上career少但是jobs遍地都是,干点啥不
行,本来这个发表考核体系就很toxic。btw,关于最近
热搜的题外话,热爱一件事就最好不要当成挣钱工具,
那样肯定会被OKR绑架,接受随时可以抽身而走才能过
的更自然一些吧。但是学术培养又很强调passion来指
引一个人入行和具有漫长的培训成本,这块也挺矛盾的
反正
#科研 #博士生精神状态
② 分享你的论文写作经验
Edited on Yesterday 19:32 广东
91万人发布 >
Say something...
164
130
12

events: Posted on social media about the importance of taste in scientific research

content.caption: A Twitter conversation discussing the pros and cons of hard length limits for academic papers, particularly in the context of CHI conferences.
content.text:
12:49
Post
174
Jessica Hullman @Jessica Huɩɩman. 55m
Yeah, that one's pretty far down my list.
No hard length limit was a bad idea imo. I want
the core idea in a reasonable # of pgs & rest in
appendix.
But my perception's also probably partly from
having gotten used to papers that formalize
ideas much more than happens at CHI.
Q1
1
Ill 61
Sauvik Das | sauvik@hci.social @scy....35m
I think hard length limits is less graceful at
places like CHI than most other "CS"
conferences because of its epistemological
diversity. CHI accepts ethnographies and
systems papers, and many things in between
and beyond
○ 2
27
24
↑
Jessica Hullman @JessicaHullman. 6m
If I was encountering a lot of deep thought-
provoking papers, I'd be less concerned. But I
see many examples that are overly long where
the contribution wasn't ambitious to begin with.
Combined w/norms like awarding 1 in 5 papers,
it starts to seem like standards are just low.
5
☐
Post your reply
Q
☑
